qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Insufficient modular reduction in p521 Scalar::reduce() produces invalid scalars
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding is in p521 scalar reduction and concerns a single-subtraction modular reduction error. The advisory concerns sm2/src/pke/encrypting.rs, where EncryptingKey::encrypt passes a byte count as a bit count to next_k, producing a 32-bit SM2 PKE nonce.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding in the run manifest identifies the advisory-described SM2 PKE nonce-generation root cause. The advisory root cause is the sm2 EncryptingKey::encrypt path passing N_BYTES, a byte length of 32, into next_k as a bit length, causing U256::try_random_bits to generate only a 32-bit ephemeral nonce. The qualifying finding instead covers a P-521 scalar reduction arithmetic bug.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-w3g8-fp6j-wvqw describes sm2/src/pke/encrypting.rs, EncryptingKey::encrypt, next_k, and U256::try_random_bits receiving 32 bits instead of 256 bits for SM2 PKE nonce k.
p521-insufficient-scalar-reduction/vulnerability_report.json describes p521/src/arithmetic/scalar.rs Reduce<U576>::reduce performing only one subtraction, with no SM2 PKE encrypt/next_k path.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No qualifying finding matches the target advisory, so Tier1, Tier2, and Tier3 do not apply.
PoC execution was skipped because the task restricts PoC runs to advisory-matched qualifying findings.
